name: Possible Command Prompt Process Hollowing
description: This prompt detects a possible process hollowing attempt involving the command prompt (`cmd.exe`). Adversaries inject malicious code into suspended and hollowed processes to evade process-based defenses. The prompt focuses on `cmd.exe` spawning legitimate-looking network utilities like `net.exe`, `nltest.exe`, or `ipconfig.exe` without the typical command-line arguments that indicate interactive use (e.g., '/c' or '/k'). This behavior can be an indicator of a malicious code injection.

prompts.prompt: Investigate the security event where the process `{image}` was spawned by the parent process `cmd.exe` (`{parent_image}`). The event occurred at `{timestamp}` on the host `{hostname}`. The command line arguments for the parent (`{parent_command}`) do not include a '/c' or '/k', which is suspicious. Analyze the command line (`{command_line}`) of the child process. Investigate the user (`{user}`) and the context of the activity. Determine if this is a legitimate process execution or a potential process hollowing attempt. Provide a summary of your findings and any recommended actions.

parameters.name: image
parameters.description: The name of the process that was spawned (e.g., net.exe, nltest.exe).

parameters.name: parent_image
parameters.description: The parent process that initiated the execution (should be cmd.exe).

parameters.name: hostname
parameters.description: The hostname where the event was detected.

parameters.name: parent_command
parameters.description: The command line of the parent process (cmd.exe).

parameters.name: command_line
parameters.description: The full command line of the child process.

parameters.name: user
parameters.description: The user account under which the process was executed.
